behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction names the affected files (packages/react-router/src/useBlocker.tsx, packages/solid-router/src/useBlocker.tsx, packages/vue-router/src/useBlocker.tsx), the type name ShouldBlockFnLocation, the error TS4023, the useBlocker hook, and the docs file. no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction describes the problem (TS4023 error when wrapping useBlocker) and affected files but does not state the fix of changing 'interface' to 'type'. A developer must reason about what change to make.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests like test_interface_converted_to_type_react assert 'type ShouldBlockFnLocation<' in content and assert 'interface ShouldBlockFnLocation<' not in content, plus test_type_alias_has_equals_sign_react checks for '> = {' syntax. An alternative correct fix (e.g., exporting the interface so it can be named, or re-exporting the type from index.tsx) would also fix TS4023 but would fail these text-matching tests.

While there are also behavioral tests (build, type-check, unit tests), the core fail_to_pass tests are all string-in-file checks with no execution.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests check that 'interface ShouldBlockFnLocation<' is NOT in the file and 'type ShouldBlockFnLocation<' IS present. A stub/no-op wouldn't satisfy these since the base code has 'interface'. The agent must actually modify the source files.

anti_cheating_measures.reason: The solution/solve.sh exists in the container at /workspace and contains the exact patch. An agent could read it directly (cat /solution/solve.sh or similar) to discover the exact fix. Additionally, the f2p tests check for literal strings ('type ShouldBlockFnLocation<') that could be matched by grepping the test files.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction requires: fixing TS4023 across three router packages and updating docs. Tests cover all three packages (react, solid, vue), build/type-check/lint, unit tests, and the docs file is modified in the solution.
